# Research Notes: Narrative Structure Techniques

## Step
- Step 1 of Plan (20260401-concurrent-01)

## Sources Used
- src-001 (Purdue OWL — Narrative Structure) — ACQ-001, passed
- src-002 (Britannica — Narrative Structure) — ACQ-001, passed

## Key Findings

### Three-Act Structure
The three-act structure divides a narrative into Setup (Act I), Confrontation (Act II), and Resolution (Act III). Act I introduces protagonist, setting, and central conflict through the inciting incident. Act II (the longest) presents escalating obstacles culminating in a crisis. Act III brings the climax and denouement. This framework remains the dominant model in commercial fiction and screenwriting (src-001, src-002).

### In Medias Res
In medias res ("into the middle of things") opens the story at a midpoint or climactic moment rather than the chronological beginning. First described by Horace in Ars Poetica (c. 19 BCE), who praised Homer for beginning the Iliad amid the Trojan War. Key characteristics: opens with action/conflict, defers exposition, relies on flashbacks, creates urgency (src-001, src-002).

### Freytag's Pyramid
Gustav Freytag developed a five-part dramatic structure: Exposition, Rising Action, Climax, Falling Action, Denouement. Originally for classical drama, now widely applied to prose fiction and screenwriting (src-001).

### Story Arcs
Story arcs trace the overall trajectory of transformation across a narrative. Common types: The Hero's Journey (Campbell), Rags to Riches, Tragedy, Rebirth (src-001).

### Structural Models from Britannica
Beyond three-act structure: Linear structure (chronological), Non-linear structure (flashbacks/prolepsis), Frame narrative (story within story), Circular structure (ends where it began). Russian Formalist distinction between fabula (story) and syuzhet (plot) (src-002).

### Historical Foundation
The study of narrative structure traces to Aristotle's Poetics (c. 335 BCE): beginning (protasis), middle (epitasis), end (catastrophe). Unity of action — every element must contribute to the whole (src-002).
